title: Authorized User Add-On Bust-Out on Thin-File Synthetic Account
narrative: A synthetic identity with 4-7 months of light payment history (card1_txn_count_90d under 15, total credit line $1,200-$2,500) suddenly adds an authorized user. Within 48-96 hours, coordinated dual-location spending begins: primary cardholder in addr1 zip making $40-$85 grocery/gas purchases (MCC 5411, 5542) while new AU initiates $300-$750 electronics transactions (MCC 5732, 5946) from addr2 located 400+ miles away (dist1>400). The AU email uses disposable domain (P_emaildomain: gmx.de, protonmail.com) registered within 14 days of card modification. Total spending velocity jumps 600-900% above baseline (card1_amt_zscore exceeds +4.5) within first week. Pattern climaxes with simultaneous $1,800-$2,400 purchases at both locations within 3-hour window, maxing available credit before bust-out. No payments posted after AU addition.
indicators: card1_txn_count_90d under 15 prior to authorized user addition, then 25+ transactions in 7 days post-addition
Dual-location spending with dist1 values exceeding 400 miles between primary and AU transactions within same 24-hour period
card1_amt_zscore jumps from baseline 0.5-1.2 to 4.5+ within 72 hours of AU activation
AU P_emaildomain from disposable/temporary provider (gmx.de, protonmail.com, guerrillamail.com) registered less than 14 days before
Simultaneous high-value transactions ($1,800-$2,400 range) from both locations within 3-hour window
Total credit line utilization increases from under 30% to 95-100% within 5-7 days of AU addition
ProductCD shift from recurring low-ticket (W: wallet topup, C: card present) to high-ticket electronics/jewelry (H: home/electronics)
summary: A synthetic identity with minimal transaction history suddenly adds an authorized user, then both cardholders begin spending simultaneously from locations 400+ miles apart—primary making small grocery purchases while the AU buys high-value electronics. Within days, spending velocity spikes 600-900% above baseline and both max out the credit line with coordinated large purchases in a 3-hour window.
reasoning: Fraudsters build a thin credit file to establish legitimacy, then add a conspirator as authorized user to double their theft capacity before abandoning the account. The geographic separation, disposable email, explosive spending increase, and synchronized final purchases reveal a planned bust-out rather than legitimate authorized user activity.
indicator_explanations: Dormant card suddenly explodes with activity right after authorized user gets added
Purchases happen in two distant cities impossibly fast after user addition
Spending amounts skyrocket far beyond normal patterns within days of AU
Authorized user email is throwaway service created right before being added
Both cardholders max out purchases at same moment from different locations
Credit line goes from responsible use to completely maxed in one week
Purchase types flip from small everyday items to expensive liquidatable goods
